context.meal: buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing
context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=5; meal=buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to reheat a cold fruit meal, consistent with intended serving temperature. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected reheating for savory meals in work contexts. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user again chose reheating for a savory meal, suggesting food temperature preference is driven more by meal type than by social context. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal with buffalo chicken bites and potato wedges THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected the hottest observed reheating level for that savory lunch, reinforcing a preference to warm hot entrées while cold foods remain unmicrowaved.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not prioritize view preservation in the observed personal setting. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve visibility toward the right-side workspace. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid blocking the social partner on the left. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve the view of the TV. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not prioritize left-side visibility in that observed work context. Across observed meals, occlusion preference is direction-specific and becomes important when there is a salient person or screen in a particular direction, but is not uniformly applied in every non-personal context.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred a comfortable, unhurried motion style. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected the more comfortable and socially gentle motion style. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN slow BECAUSE the user again preferred a comfortable pace while attending to a front-facing screen. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected a time-efficient interaction style in that work context. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN medium BECAUSE the user still preferred some efficiency while working, but less aggressively than in the prior right-laptop work meal. Across observed contexts, work settings shift the user toward faster operation, while personal, social, and TV-viewing settings favor slower movement.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected a narrower bite-acquisition style for that food. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets and broccoli meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style for that meal. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user again selected the higher-success acquisition style for bite-sized savory items. Across observed meals, skewering preference appears food-dependent rather than driven by social, work, or TV context alone.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user chose to avoid extra confirmation overhead. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred reduced interaction burden and greater automation. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the user still chose to skip explicit bite-acquisition confirmation even in a social setting. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred not to interrupt the meal with extra web confirmation. Across observed contexts, the user consistently prefers not to add this extra web confirmation step.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's selected and physically appropriate transfer mode is inside mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal readiness cues. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN speech BECAUSE the user accepted speech-only cues in that efficiency-oriented work context. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal cues there. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user again chose multimodal signaling. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user again selected multimodal readiness cues. Across observed meals, speech + LED is the dominant pattern, with speech-only appearing in one strongly efficiency-oriented work context.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and explicitly preferred an active but low-effort readiness signal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user chose a highly automated, low-interruption initiation method in that work context. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary low-effort signaling rather than full automation. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user still preferred voluntary control despite the social setting. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred low-effort voluntary control rather than automatic initiation. Across observed contexts, button use is physically infeasible, and the user generally favors open mouth, with autocontinue used selectively in a strongly efficiency-oriented work context.

IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and selected a low-effort voluntary readiness cue. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user preferred automatic progression for drink transfer initiation in that work context. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary readiness signaling there. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred voluntary low-effort control. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred voluntary readiness signaling. Across observed contexts, button use is physically infeasible, and drinking initiation mirrors feeding: usually open mouth, with autocontinue reserved for a highly efficiency-oriented work context.

IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user preferred automatic progression for mouth-wiping initiation across observed work meals. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation. Across observed contexts, the user consistently prefers higher automation for wiping initiation.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal completion cues. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN speech BECAUSE the user accepted speech-only completion cues in that work context. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cues there. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user again chose multimodal completion signaling. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user again selected multimodal completion cues. Across observed meals, speech + LED is the dominant pattern, with speech-only appearing in one efficiency-oriented work context.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and selected the reliable low-effort completion detection method for feeding.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and selected perception-based completion detection for drinking.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and selected perception-based completion detection for mouth wiping.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user chose not to add extra retraction motions. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly prioritized efficiency over additional retraction. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the user still preferred to reduce meal time rather than retract between tasks. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly chose retraction while attending to a front-facing screen, consistent with preserving visibility in that context. Across observed meals, the user usually prefers not to retract, but may enable retraction when front-view access to a screen is salient.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was unnecessary and not selected. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ketchup or another available dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE in the observed work meal with ketchup the user explicitly chose not to add dipping. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal without a selected dipping sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user again chose no dipping. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but reduced amount, consistent with a mess-conscious social preference. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ranch dressing THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to dip despite sauce availability. Across observed meals, the user avoids dipping by default, with one observed exception of restrained dipping in a social setting.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred a longer pause before automatic continuation. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred rapid automatic continuation and a time-efficient workflow in work settings. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a longer wait, suggesting more tolerance for pauses in social interaction than in work-focused meals. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user again preferred a longer wait rather than rapid automatic progression. Across observed contexts, the user prefers longer waits in personal, social, and TV-viewing settings, with short waits reserved for efficiency-oriented work contexts.
